faq.heading: Your questions, answered
faq.sub: The six questions buyers ask us most, with honest answers from our team.

items.q: Can foreigners own property in the Dominican Republic?
items.a: Yes. Foreigners have the same property rights as Dominican citizens and can own land and real estate outright in their own name — no residency required.

items.q: What taxes do I pay when buying property?
items.a: A one-time 3% transfer tax (Impuesto de Transferencia) at closing, plus an annual 1% property tax (IPI) on value above the exemption threshold. CONFOTUR-approved projects can waive these.

items.q: How long does the buying process take?
items.a: Typically 30–60 days from accepted offer to title transfer, with due diligence being the longest phase at roughly 30 days.

items.q: Do I need a Dominican attorney?
items.a: Strongly recommended. A local real estate attorney handles title searches, due diligence, and the closing — protecting you from boundary disputes and title defects.

items.q: What is residency and do I need it to buy property?
items.a: You do NOT need residency to buy. Residency (including the Law 171-07 investor/retiree route) offers tax benefits and the right to live in the DR long-term, but ownership is open to all foreigners.

items.q: Can I rent out my property when I'm not using it?
items.a: Yes. Short- and long-term rentals are common, and I♥DR Realty's booking tools let owners manage availability and payments directly.
